transcript.content: 每次功能改完测试完，自动提交github 每次改完测试完自动部署到本地openclaw ，需要重启openclaw的话，那就自动重启下（可以通过openclaw gateway restart 来重启） 不要硬编码； 有新功能后要及时更新文档，文档要简洁明了，方便后续维护和使用； 测试要覆盖到新功能，测试用例要清晰明了，方便后续维护和使用； 如果context大小超过50%，给个提醒，建议删除过时的内容，保持简洁明了； 如果需要我来测试的话，大字体告诉我要怎么做，需要简明扼要； 目标： 在新的 Codex 线程里重做体感测试，确认 Context Minor GC 现在是否更薄、更稳、切题后更明显收缩。 已完成： - 已修复 timeout 伪装成 continue 的问题；现在 timeout 会明确显示 decision_timeout。 - 已修复 [GC-*] 手工测试被 assistant 指挥话术污染的问题；现在会按 case 前缀只抽同 case 消息。 - 已修复 small working set 被 summary-first 膨胀的问题；raw keep 更轻时会退回 raw context。 - 已把 codex-minor-gc-current-editor-update-after-detour 和 codex-minor-gc-zh-dense-multitopic-return 收进固定回归。 - 最新 codex live eval 已全绿：cases=6, minor-gc passed=6, false activations=0, missed activations=0。 进行中： - 现在要做的是“新线程真实体感测试”，不是继续改代码。 - 需要你直接引导我做最短的 2 组手工 case，并在每组结束后读 gc 指标。 涉及文件： - /Users/redcreen/Project/unified-memory-core/src/structured-decision-runner.js - /Users/redcree...

记忆已恢复，当前我会按“只做新线程体感测试，不改代码”的约束继续。 关键信息我已经对齐： - 当前目标是新 Codex 线程里做 2 组最短手工 case，验证 Context Minor GC 是否更薄、更稳、切题后更明显收缩。 - 顺序固定：先跑“中文多轮切题 case”，再跑“旧值被新值覆盖后再回问”的 case。 - 每组结束后都要执行一次 `npm run codex:vscode:gc --silent`，重点看…

decision.archive_summary: Active task remains the same: do not change code; guide the user through two shortest manual Context Minor GC tests in a new Codex thread, in fixed order, and read GC metrics after each case. Durable user rules/preferences from t1 should persist as a compact pin: auto-commit/deploy workflow expectations, concise docs/tests, no hardcoding, remind when context exceeds 50%, and give large-font concise user-testing instructions when user action is needed.
decision.reasoning_summary: The latest user turn ('开始') is a direct continuation of the still-open manual testing task established in t1 and clarified in t2. The next turn depends on the raw context of both t1 and t2, so nothing should be evicted from the working set yet. t1 also contains durable operating preferences and repo-specific working rules worth preserving as a semantic pin for later even if raw turns are trimmed in a future switch/resolve.

The next turn depends on the raw context of both t1 and t2, so nothing should be evicted from the working set yet. t1 also contains durable operating preferences and repo-specific working rules worth preserving as a semantic pin for later even if raw turns are trimmed in a future switch/resolve.
